leagueBriefing: Duke swept the season series over North Carolina to highlight a wild final Saturday, while coaching legends John Calipari and Scott Drew hit the 900 and 500 career win milestones, respectively.

games.headline: McCarty’s second-half surge clinches No. 2 seed for Houston
games.brief: Houston overcame a sluggish start and a 13-point first-half deficit to rally past Oklahoma State in Stillwater. Redshirt freshman Chase McCarty was the catalyst, exploding for 20 points—including six triples—to silence the Cowboys' crowd. The victory officially locks the Cougars into the No. 2 seed and a double-bye for the Big 12 Tournament.
bullets: Chase McCarty scored 14 of his game-high 20 points in the second half.
Kingston Flemings neared a triple-double with 13 points, 5 rebounds, and 9 assists.
Houston secured the No. 2 seed and a quarterfinal spot in the Big 12 Tournament.
Oklahoma State's Anthony Roy led the Cowboys with 18 points.
The Cougars outscored the Cowboys 45-34 across the final 20 minutes.
games.context: Houston secures a double-bye in the Big 12 Tournament, while Oklahoma State falls to the first round.

games.headline: No. 13 Virginia fends off rival Hokies to protect home court
games.brief: Virginia utilized a suffocating first-half defense and clinical free-throw shooting to hold off a late Virginia Tech charge. After jumping out to a 12-0 lead, the Cavaliers maintained control despite the Hokies tying the game midway through the second half. For Virginia Tech, the loss is a significant blow to their NCAA Tournament bubble hopes on the final day of the regular season.
bullets: Ugo Onyenso and Malik Thomas led the Cavaliers with 16 points each.
Virginia shot a near-perfect 15-of-17 (88.2%) from the free-throw line.
Ben Hammond led the Hokies with 21 points but missed two late free throws.
Virginia Tech missed 8 of their first 9 attempts from 3-point range.
The Cavaliers had already clinched the No. 2 seed in the ACC Tournament.
games.context: Virginia enters the postseason as a top-2 seed while Virginia Tech's bubble status becomes precarious.

games.headline: Calipari hits 900 wins as Arkansas survives overtime thriller in Columbia
games.brief: John Calipari became just the fifth D-I men’s coach to reach 900 career wins as his Razorbacks outlasted Missouri in overtime. Despite missing scoring leader Darius Acuff Jr., Meleek Thomas stepped up with 28 points. Trevon Brazile provided the ultimate heartbreak for his former team, draining the go-ahead 3-pointer with 22 seconds left in the extra period.
bullets: John Calipari reaches the 900-win milestone with the overtime victory.
Meleek Thomas led all scorers with 28 points.
Missouri’s Mark Mitchell scored a career-high 32 points in the loss.
Trevon Brazile hit the deciding 3-pointer against his former school.
Malique Ewin sealed the game with four straight free throws in the final 12 seconds.
games.context: A historic night for Calipari while Arkansas clinches an SEC Tournament quarterfinal bye.

games.headline: Hurley ejected in final second as Marquette denies UConn title share
games.brief: Marquette pulled off a defensive masterpiece in Milwaukee, holding No. 4 UConn to just 12.5% from deep. The game ended in high drama when Dan Hurley was ejected after arguing a non-foul call on the final play. The win secures Marquette's momentum heading into the Big East Tournament and keeps UConn from grabbing a share of the regular-season crown.
bullets: Dan Hurley was ejected with 1.0 seconds left and later fined $25,000.
UConn shot a dismal 3-of-24 from 3-point range.
Nigel James Jr. led the Golden Eagles with 19 points.
UConn's Silas Demary Jr. scored 17 in the losing effort.
The loss hands the outright Big East No. 1 seed to St. John's.
games.context: St. John's wins the Big East outright as UConn settles for the No. 2 seed.

games.headline: Cyclones use massive 24-0 second-half run to bury Arizona State
games.brief: Iowa State turned a four-point deficit into a blowout in the blink of an eye, unleashing a 24-0 run that left Arizona State shell-shocked. The Cyclones’ high-pressure defense forced a season-high 23 turnovers, while the offense hummed with three players reaching 16 points. It was the largest single run in the TJ Otzelberger era and a reminder of how dangerous Hilton Coliseum is for visitors.
bullets: Iowa State's 24-0 run lasted nearly nine minutes of game time.
Joshua Jefferson, Tamin Lipsey, and Milan Momcilovic all scored 16 points.
The Cyclones forced Arizona State into a season-high 23 turnovers.
ASU went nearly 11 minutes without a made field goal in the second half.
Iowa State finished with 49 second-half points to ASU's 24.
games.context: Iowa State enters the Big 12 Tournament as a dangerous No. 5 seed.

games.headline: Darryn Peterson dominates in Allen Fieldhouse finale as Jayhawks roll
games.brief: No. 14 Kansas celebrated Senior Day in style, crushing in-state rival Kansas State behind 27 points from freshman phenom Darryn Peterson. The Jayhawks showcased an offensive clinic to clinch a double-bye in the Big 12 Tournament, extending their home winning streak over the Wildcats to 20 years. For a team that had struggled lately, the 104-point explosion was a loud message to the rest of the conference.
bullets: Freshman Darryn Peterson scored 27 points on efficient 10-of-15 shooting.
Kansas secured their 43rd consecutive Senior Day victory.
Melvin Council Jr. flirted with a triple-double (17 pts, 8 reb, 10 ast).
The Jayhawks clinched a double-bye for next week’s conference tournament.
P.J. Haggerty and Nate Johnson combined for 42 points for the Wildcats.
games.context: Kansas clinches a double-bye while K-State falls to the No. 15 seed.

games.headline: Wooley’s late triple stuns No. 22 Miami as Louisville ends road drought
games.brief: Louisville pulled off a stunner in Coral Gables, snapping a 14-game road losing streak against ranked opponents. Adrian Wooley knocked down the go-ahead 3-pointer with 18 seconds left to salvage a win after the Cardinals blew a 12-point lead. Miami’s bid for a school-record 25th regular-season win fell short when a last-second heave rimmed out.
bullets: Louisville snapped a road losing streak vs. ranked teams dating back to 2020.
Ryan Conwell paced the Cardinals with 24 points.
Adrian Wooley hit the game-winning 3-pointer with 18.4 seconds remaining.
Tre Donaldson scored a game-high 25 points for the Hurricanes.
Miami had already secured the No. 3 seed in the ACC Tournament.
games.context: Louisville gains major momentum heading into the ACC Tournament's second round.

games.headline: Vanderbilt controls rivalry clash to sweep No. 23 Tennessee
games.brief: Vanderbilt jumped out to a 15-2 lead and never looked back, fending off a furious 60-point second-half surge from the Volunteers. Tyler Tanner’s 25 points kept the Commodores ahead as they completed a crucial regular-season sweep of their rivals. Tennessee struggled with early turnovers and played without star Nate Ament, falling just short despite a late rally.
bullets: Tyler Tanner led the Commodores with 25 points.
Tennessee scored 60 points in the second half but couldn't erase the early deficit.
The Volunteers committed 9 turnovers in the first half alone.
Vanderbilt won despite not making a single 3-pointer in the game.
Nate Ament missed the game for Tennessee with a high ankle sprain.
games.context: Both teams finish 11-7 in SEC play heading into the tournament in Nashville.

games.headline: No. 5 Florida sweeps Kentucky to finish regular season on 11-game win streak
games.brief: The defending national champion Florida Gators showed no signs of slowing down, walking into Rupp Arena and never trailing. Behind 20 points from Thomas Haugh, the Gators secured an 11th straight victory and a regular-season sweep of the Wildcats. Florida enters the SEC Tournament as the No. 1 seed, winning the conference by a massive three-game margin.
bullets: Thomas Haugh led the Gators with 20 points and 9 rebounds.
Florida opened the game with an 11-0 run and never surrendered the lead.
The Gators won the SEC regular-season title by three games.
Otega Oweh scored 28 points for Kentucky in his Senior Day finale.
Florida has now won 11 straight games heading into the postseason.
games.context: Florida enters the SEC Tournament as the clear No. 1 seed and title favorite.

games.headline: Wisconsin sets school record for 3s to stun Purdue at home
games.brief: In a game for the ages, Wisconsin set a school road record with 18 three-pointers to outgun No. 15 Purdue. John Blackwell and Nick Boyd combined for 48 points as the Badgers thwarted every Boilermaker run. The loss was a historic anomaly for Purdue, who hadn't lost a game while scoring 90+ points since 1987.
bullets: Wisconsin made a school-record 18 three-pointers on 34 attempts.
John Blackwell scored 25 points; Nick Boyd added 23.
Fletcher Loyer broke Carsen Edwards' Purdue record for career 3-pointers.
The Badgers led by 5 at halftime and never let Purdue take a permanent second-half lead.
Purdue shot over 50% but couldn't match Wisconsin's perimeter volume.
games.context: Wisconsin clinches its 22nd win and momentum for the Big Ten Tournament.

games.headline: George Mason dismantles No. 25 Saint Louis in stunning upset
games.brief: George Mason saved their best for last, routing first-place Saint Louis by nearly 30 points. The Billikens were hampered early when star center Robbie Avila landed in foul trouble, and the Patriots capitalized with a 17-0 second-half run. It was a statement win for George Mason, who hadn't beaten a ranked opponent in over two years.
bullets: Jahari Long led the Patriots with 21 points and 9 assists.
George Mason shot a scorching 73.1% from the field in the second half.
Saint Louis star Robbie Avila was held to just 2 points in 12 minutes due to fouls.
The Patriots' 17-0 run in the second half put the game out of reach.
Saint Louis had already clinched the No. 1 seed in the A-10 Tournament.
games.context: George Mason secures a confidence-boosting win while SLU enters the A-10 tourney on a flat note.

games.headline: Duke overwhelms short-handed Heels with 24-2 second-half blast
games.brief: No. 1 Duke completed the season sweep of North Carolina, but the victory was bittersweet as both teams saw key players exit with injuries. Cameron Boozer was unstoppable with a 26-point double-double, anchoring a massive second-half run that turned a one-possession game into a blowout. UNC played without Caleb Wilson, who was lost for the season to a thumb injury just 48 hours prior.
bullets: Cameron Boozer recorded 26 points, 15 rebounds, and 5 assists.
Duke used a 24-2 run to seal the victory in the second half.
UNC's Caleb Wilson is officially out for the season with a broken thumb.
Duke starting guard Caleb Foster left the game with a foot injury.
The Blue Devils finished the ACC regular season with 17-1 record.
games.context: Duke takes the top overall seed into the ACC Tournament as title favorites.

games.headline: Alabama secures No. 2 seed with wire-to-wire rivalry win over Auburn
games.brief: Alabama left no doubt in their regular-season finale, jumping out to a fast start and never trailing against rival Auburn. Labaron Philon and Aden Holloway each poured in 21 points to lead a Tide offense that built a lead as large as 28. The win locks Alabama into the double-bye as the SEC's No. 2 seed, while Auburn's late-season slide continues.
bullets: Labaron Philon reached his 1,000th career point during his 21-point night.
Alabama opened the game making their first six consecutive shots.
The Crimson Tide outscored the Tigers by double digits in the second-half run.
Kevin Overton led Auburn with 24 points in a losing effort.
Alabama clinches the No. 2 seed in next week's SEC Tournament.
games.context: Alabama locks in a double-bye while Auburn falls to 7-11 in conference play.

games.headline: Robert Wright III rallies BYU to top-10 upset of Texas Tech
games.brief: BYU snapped a three-game skid in spectacular fashion, riding a massive second half from freshman Robert Wright III to stun No. 10 Texas Tech. Wright scored 23 of his 27 points after the break, leading a Cougar attack that dominated the paint. Texas Tech had scorched the nets with 12 first-half triples but couldn't keep pace once BYU’s interior game took over late.
bullets: Robert Wright III scored 23 of his 27 points in the second half.
BYU outscored Texas Tech 48-14 in points in the paint.
Texas Tech made 12 of 19 from 3-point range in the first half alone.
AJ Dybantsa added 21 points and 6 rebounds for the Cougars.
BYU snapped a three-game losing streak with the victory.
games.context: BYU secures a momentum-shifting win; Texas Tech retains the No. 4 seed and a double-bye.

games.headline: No. 2 Arizona sets school record for wins in comeback over Colorado
games.brief: Arizona cemented its place as a national title contender by setting a program record with 29 regular-season wins. After trailing by 11 early, freshmen Brayden Burries and Koa Peat took over, combining for 56 points to spoil Colorado's upset bid. The Wildcats entered the night having already clinched the Big 12 title, but the comeback win in Boulder showed they have no plans of slowing down.
bullets: Brayden Burries scored 22 of his 31 points in the second half.
Koa Peat shot 12-of-15 from the field to finish with 25 points.
Arizona’s 29 regular-season wins are the most in program history.
Colorado freshman Isaiah Johnson set a school record with 28 points.
The Wildcats rallied from a 36-25 deficit late in the first half.
games.context: Arizona enters the Big 12 Tournament as the No. 1 seed and national title favorite.

games.headline: Aggies outlast LSU in triple-overtime marathon to close SEC play
games.brief: In one of the longest games of the season, Texas A&M survived a three-overtime battle in Baton Rouge to secure the No. 6 seed in the SEC. Rashaun Agee and Rylan Griffen both went the distance, combining for 50 points and 18 rebounds in a game that saw LSU lead by as many as 15. The Tigers fought to the end despite their place at the bottom of the standings, but the Aggies' clutch shooting in the third extra period proved the difference.
bullets: Rashaun Agee posted 26 points and 11 rebounds in 47 minutes of play.
Rylan Griffen added 24 points and a game-high 6 steals.
The game featured three overtimes after being tied at 70 at the end of regulation.
LSU's Mike Nwoko recorded a double-double (16 pts, 13 reb).
Texas A&M clinched the No. 6 seed for the SEC Tournament.
games.context: Texas A&M secures a Thursday start in Nashville; LSU finishes last in the SEC.

games.headline: Mercyhurst advances to NEC final despite NCAA ineligibility
games.brief: Mercyhurst continued its improbable postseason run, holding off Stonehill to reach the NEC Tournament final. However, due to NCAA reclassification rules, the Lakers are ineligible for the Big Dance, meaning the auto-bid was decided in the other semifinal. Cameron Johnson paced the Lakers with 17 points in a game that was a defensive struggle from start to finish.
bullets: Cameron Johnson led Mercyhurst with 17 points.
The Lakers are ineligible for the NCAA Tournament due to D-I transition rules.
Stonehill’s Davante Hackett scored a game-high 21 points.
Mercyhurst held Stonehill to just 51 points in the victory.
The NEC auto-bid was awarded to the LIU-Wagner winner as a result of the Lakers' status.
games.context: Mercyhurst reaches a title game they cannot use to enter the NCAA Tournament.

games.headline: Bizjack’s 19 points lead Butler to road win over Blue Demons
games.brief: Butler leaned on balanced scoring and a strong performance from Finley Bizjack to take down DePaul in Chicago. The Bulldogs overcame a monster 24-point double-double from N.J. Benson by forcing turnovers and executing late. Both teams now head to the Big East Tournament looking for a fresh start after finishing the regular season at .500 overall.
bullets: Finley Bizjack led Butler with 19 points.
Efeosa Oliogu-Elabor added 15 points and 4 steals for the Bulldogs.
DePaul's N.J. Benson had a massive line of 24 points, 10 rebounds, and 3 blocks.
Butler shot nearly 50% from the floor to secure the road victory.
Both teams finished the regular season with 16-15 overall records.
games.context: Both teams prepare for the Big East Tournament opening round in New York.

games.headline: Brennan’s double-double anchors Villanova's wire-to-wire win over Xavier
games.brief: Villanova controlled the pace from the opening tip, never trailing in a double-digit win over Xavier. Duke Brennan was a force inside, tallying 20 points and 13 rebounds to lead the Wildcats. Xavier’s Jovan Milicevic tried to keep the Musketeers close with 21 points, but Villanova’s hot shooting from the perimeter (including six 3s from Tyler Perkins) kept the game out of reach.
bullets: Duke Brennan posted 20 points and 13 rebounds.
Tyler Perkins made 6 of 9 attempts from 3-point range.
Villanova took the lead 16 minutes into the first half and never looked back.
Jovan Milicevic led Xavier with 21 points in the loss.
The Wildcats shot 44 points in the first half to establish a 12-point cushion.
games.context: Villanova finishes the Big East schedule at 15-5, heading into the tourney as a top contender.

games.headline: Payne’s 24 points spark Boston College comeback over Irish
games.brief: Boston College closed out the ACC regular season with a gritty comeback win, riding a career night from Fred Payne. The Eagles struggled early, going scoreless for the final nine minutes of the first half, but exploded for an 11-0 run late in the game to take control. Despite the win, the Eagles were denied a spot in the conference tournament after tiebreakers fell in favor of Pittsburgh.
bullets: Fred Payne led the Eagles with 24 points.
Boston College made 31 of 41 free-throw attempts in the victory.
The Eagles went nearly 11 minutes (including the half) without a made field goal.
Notre Dame's Jalen Haralson scored 21 points in his freshman finale.
Both teams finished tied for 16th in the ACC standings.
games.context: The win wasn't enough to save BC's season as they miss the ACC Tournament field.

games.headline: Clemson rallies late to hand Georgia Tech 12th straight loss
games.brief: Clemson avoided a regular-season finale disaster, rallying from a halftime deficit to fend off a winless-in-weeks Georgia Tech squad. Butta Johnson and Nick Davidson hit timely buckets down the stretch to secure the No. 5 seed for the Tigers. The Yellow Jackets fought hard behind Jaeden Mustaf’s 28 points, but ultimately couldn't snap their double-digit losing streak before their season came to an end.
bullets: Butta Johnson and Nick Davidson each scored 15 points for Clemson.
Georgia Tech finishes the season on a 12-game losing streak.
Jaeden Mustaf led all scorers with 28 points for the Jackets.
Clemson outscored Georgia Tech 46-37 in the second half.
The Tigers secured the No. 5 seed in the ACC Tournament.
games.context: Clemson gains momentum for Charlotte while Georgia Tech heads into the offseason.

games.headline: Bulldogs use monstrous 34-2 second-half run to embarrass Delaware
games.brief: Louisiana Tech turned a competitive game into a laugher with a jaw-dropping 34-2 run in the second half. The Bulldogs' defense completely suffocated Delaware, holding them to just 13 points after the break. Avery Thomas and Kaden Cooper led the charge as Louisiana Tech built a lead that peaked at 45 points on the Blue Hens' home court.
bullets: Louisiana Tech outscored Delaware 44-13 in the second half.
Avery Thomas led the Bulldogs with 20 points and 6 rebounds.
Kaden Cooper provided 17 points and 6 steals off the bench.
The Bulldogs' 34-2 run was one of the largest in C-USA history.
Delaware was held to just 38 points total for the game.
games.context: Louisiana Tech enters the C-USA tourney with significant defensive momentum.

games.headline: Valentine’s 23 points power UMBC into America East semifinals
games.brief: UMBC controlled their America East quarterfinal matchup from the jump, never trailing against New Hampshire. Anthony Valentine was the star of the show, pouring in 23 points to lead the Retrievers into the next round. UNH got a double-double from Belal El Shakery, but they couldn't overcome a UMBC squad that seemed to have an answer for every run.
bullets: Anthony Valentine scored a team-high 23 points.
UMBC led for 39:44 of the 40-minute contest.
Belal El Shakery led UNH with 20 points and 10 rebounds.
The Retrievers outscored the Wildcats by 11 in the second half.
UMBC advances to face UMass Lowell in the semifinals.
games.context: UMBC moves one step closer to an America East title and NCAA berth.

games.headline: Ajogbor’s perfect shooting night leads St. Joe’s to sixth straight win
games.brief: Justice Ajogbor was an immovable force in Philadelphia, going a perfect 8-for-8 from the field to lead Saint Joseph's past rival La Salle. The Hawks used a 9-0 second-half run to break open a close game and secure their sixth consecutive victory. Derek Simpson added a near triple-double as the Hawks solidified their status as the hottest team in the Atlantic 10.
bullets: Justice Ajogbor scored 20 points on a perfect 8-of-8 shooting performance.
Derek Simpson tallied 16 points, 7 rebounds, and 9 assists.
The Hawks have now won six straight games heading into the postseason.
La Salle’s Rob Dockery led all scorers with 25 points.
St. Joe's outscored La Salle 43-30 in the final 20 minutes.
games.context: St. Joe's enters the A-10 tournament as the league's most dangerous dark horse.

games.headline: Meechie Johnson’s deep buzzer-beater stuns Ole Miss
games.brief: In a finish that will live in Gamecocks lore, Meechie Johnson drained a massive 3-pointer from well beyond the arc as the buzzer sounded to break a late tie in Oxford. South Carolina had led for most of the second half before a Rebels rally tied it in the final minute. The shot ended a five-minute field goal drought for the Gamecocks and handed Ole Miss their 12th loss in 13 games.
bullets: Meechie Johnson hit the game-winning 3-pointer as time expired.
South Carolina led 34-28 at the half.
Ole Miss struggled mightily from deep, shooting just 1-of-14 from 3-point range.
Malik Dia led the Rebels with 22 points.
The win gives South Carolina momentum heading into a Wednesday SEC tourney start.
games.context: The Gamecocks get a highlight-reel finish to end a difficult regular season.

games.headline: LIU punches first ticket to the Big Dance with NEC semifinal win
games.brief: The LIU Sharks are going to the NCAA Tournament. Despite having to play in the NEC final on Tuesday, LIU secured the conference's automatic bid because their final opponent, Mercyhurst, is currently ineligible for the D-I postseason. Greg Gordon and Jamal Fuller led the way in a physical semifinal that was tied at the half before the Sharks' defense took over late.
bullets: LIU clinches the NEC automatic bid to the NCAA Tournament.
Greg Gordon led the Sharks with 19 points and 4 steals.
Jamal Fuller added 17 points and a dominant 5 blocks.
The game was tied 23-23 at halftime before LIU pulled away.
Wagner’s Nick Jones led all scorers with 27 points.
games.context: LIU becomes the first team in the nation to officially secure a 2026 NCAA Tournament berth.

games.headline: Hurley’s 29-point outburst sends Vermont to America East semis
games.brief: Vermont showed why they are the perennial power of the America East, dismantling Bryant to reach the conference semifinals. TJ Hurley was unstoppable, scoring 24 of his 29 points in a dominant second half. Bryant kept it close early, but a 12-0 Catamounts run after the break turned the game into a comfortable victory for the home side.
bullets: TJ Hurley scored a game-high 29 points on 9-of-12 shooting.
Vermont used a 12-0 second-half run to break the game open.
Keegan Harvey led Bryant with 21 points in the loss.
The Catamounts held Bryant to just 27 points in the first half.
Vermont advances to play NJIT in the semifinals on Tuesday.
games.context: The Catamounts continue their march toward another potential NCAA bid.

games.headline: Cornell explodes for 111 points in track-meet win over Dartmouth
games.brief: In a game that felt more like an NBA All-Star contest, Cornell reached the 111-point mark to overwhelm Dartmouth in Hanover. Jake Fiegen was clinical from deep, leading an offense that saw three players reach double figures. The Big Green tried to keep pace behind 21-point nights from McNamee and Williams, but they simply couldn't stop the Big Red's transition attack.
bullets: Cornell scored 111 points, one of the highest totals in Ivy League play this year.
Jake Fiegen scored 22 points, hitting 5 of 7 from 3-point range.
Dartmouth had two players score 21 points each (McNamee and Williams).
The Big Red finished the regular season with an 8-6 Ivy League record.
Cornell shot over 50% from the field in the high-scoring affair.
games.context: Cornell finishes with a winning Ivy record as they head into the conference tournament.

games.headline: Dotson carries Loyola Chicago past Revolutionaries
games.brief: Loyola Chicago ground out a hard-fought Atlantic 10 win behind a stellar second half from Kayde Dotson. The Ramblers led by four at the break and relied on Dotson’s 17 points and playmaking to fend off George Washington’s comeback attempts. The win provides a needed boost for a Ramblers squad that has struggled to find consistency in conference play.
bullets: Kayde Dotson scored 12 of his 17 points in the second half.
Xavier Amos added 15 points and 8 rebounds for the Ramblers.
Garrett Johnson led George Washington with 17 points.
Loyola Chicago outscored GW in both halves to secure the win.
The Ramblers improved to 4-14 in A-10 play to end the regular season.
games.context: Both teams head to Pittsburgh for the A-10 Tournament starting Wednesday.

games.headline: Fordham shuts down Rhode Island with stifling second-half defense
games.brief: Fordham overcame a halftime deficit by locking down defensively in the second half, holding Rhode Island to just 21 points after the break. Dejour Reaves led the way with 17 points, but it was Rikus Schulte’s double-double and second-half scoring that truly turned the tide. The win marks a positive end to the regular season for the Rams as they prepare for the A-10 Tournament.
bullets: Dejour Reaves led Fordham with 17 points, going 8-for-8 from the line.
Rikus Schulte recorded 14 points and 13 rebounds.
Fordham held Rhode Island to 21 points in the second half.
Jahmere Tripp paced URI with 18 points.
The game saw five lead changes in the second half before Fordham pulled away.
games.context: Fordham earns the No. 8 seed for the conference tournament.

games.headline: Duquesne erases 21-point halftime deficit to stun Richmond at the buzzer
games.brief: In the comeback of the year in the Atlantic 10, Duquesne rallied from 21 points down at halftime to beat Richmond. Alex Williams ignited the second-half surge with 13 points, but the hero was David Dixon, who crashed the glass for a game-winning putback with just one second remaining. The Dukes outscored the Spiders by 23 in the second half to complete the miraculous turnaround.
bullets: Duquesne trailed 49-28 at halftime before rallying.
David Dixon scored the game-winning putback with 1.0 seconds left.
Alex Williams led the Dukes with 22 points.
The Dukes outscored the Spiders 51-28 in the second half.
Richmond’s AJ Lopez led the Spiders with 15 points in the loss.
games.context: Duquesne enters the postseason with ultimate momentum after an all-time comeback.

games.headline: TCU finishes with program-record 11 Big 12 wins by fending off Bearcats
games.brief: TCU made history in Fort Worth, securing their fifth straight win and setting a new program high for conference victories. Jayden Pierre led a balanced Horned Frogs attack that pulled away in the second half with a 9-0 run. Cincinnati fought hard on the boards behind Baba Miller’s 14 rebounds, but they couldn't overcome TCU's efficient transition game and late-game free-throw accuracy.
bullets: TCU set a program record with 11 Big 12 Conference wins.
Jayden Pierre scored a team-high 18 points.
The Horned Frogs have won five consecutive games heading into the postseason.
Cincinnati’s Day Day Thomas led all scorers with 19 points.
TCU secured the No. 6 seed in the Big 12 Tournament.
games.context: TCU enters the Big 12 Tournament as one of the hottest teams in the conference.

games.headline: Wiggins’ career night leads FSU past SMU to end regular season
games.brief: Florida State closed the regular season on a high note, leading wire-to-wire in the second half to sink SMU. Chauncey Wiggins was unstoppable, dropping a career-high 31 points and connecting on six triples. SMU’s Boopie Miller tried to match him with a 32-point career high of his own, but the Mustangs’ foul trouble and FSU’s 52% shooting proved too much to overcome.
bullets: Chauncey Wiggins scored a career-high 31 points and 9 rebounds.
Boopie Miller scored a career-high 32 points for SMU in the loss.
FSU used a 16-6 run to close the first half with a 45-27 lead.
Two SMU players (Yigitoglu and Washington) fouled out in the second half.
The Seminoles have won three straight games heading into the ACC Tournament.
games.context: FSU earns the No. 8 seed and a first-round bye in the ACC Tournament.

games.headline: Yale crushes Princeton to finish 11-3 in Ivy League play
games.brief: Yale made a loud statement in New Haven, routing Princeton to finish the regular season at 23-5. Nick Townsend led a balanced Bulldogs attack with 17 points as Yale dominated nearly every facet of the game. Princeton struggled to find any rhythm offensively, finishing with only 53 points as they dropped to 9-20 on the year.
bullets: Nick Townsend paced Yale with 17 points and 7 rebounds.
Yale finished the Ivy League regular season with an 11-3 record.
Princeton was held to just 53 points, their lowest total in several weeks.
The Bulldogs outscored the Tigers by double digits in both halves.
Yale enters the postseason with 23 total wins on the year.
games.context: Yale enters the Ivy League Tournament as a heavy favorite.

games.headline: Cleveland’s all-around brilliance leads Liberty over Bearkats
games.brief: Liberty fended off a tough Sam Houston squad behind a masterpiece from Zach Cleveland, who flirted with a triple-double. The Flames led by 10 at the break and regained control late after the Bearkats pulled even in the second half. Sam Houston dominated the boards behind Kashie Natt’s 16 rebounds, but Liberty’s efficient offense proved more consistent down the stretch.
bullets: Zach Cleveland finished with 19 points, 9 rebounds, and 9 assists.
Liberty finished the C-USA regular season with a 17-3 record.
Kashie Natt grabbed 16 rebounds and scored 11 points for Sam Houston.
The Flames used an early 41-31 lead to set the tone.
Liberty's Zach Cleveland scored the go-ahead layup with 1:39 remaining.
games.context: Liberty finishes as a top-tier contender in Conference USA.

games.headline: Okorie’s historic 33 points lead Stanford to upset of NC State
games.brief: Stanford freshman Ebuka Okorie made ACC history, dropping 33 points to match an all-time freshman record in a thrilling win over NC State. The Cardinal shot a blistering 62% in the second half to erase a deficit and fend off the Wolfpack’s late comeback attempt. For NC State, the loss is their fourth straight, sending them into the postseason trending in the wrong direction.
bullets: Ebuka Okorie scored a career-high 33 points, matching the ACC freshman record for 30-point games.
Stanford has won four consecutive games heading into the ACC Tournament.
NC State’s Paul McNeil Jr. hit a 3-pointer at the buzzer to make it a one-point game.
The Cardinal shot 18-of-29 (62%) in the second half.
Ven-Allen Lubin led NC State with 17 points and 11 rebounds.
games.context: Stanford enters the ACC tourney on a heater, while NC State faces a crisis of momentum.

games.headline: Drexel wire-to-wire win sends Northeastern to 12th straight loss
games.brief: Drexel took control of their CAA Tournament matchup from the opening minutes, never trailing against a struggling Northeastern team. Eli Beard paced the Dragons with 21 points, while a 39-22 halftime lead provided enough cushion to survive a late Huskies rally. Northeastern’s Mike Loughnane put up a heroic 29 points, but it wasn't enough to end their double-digit losing streak.
bullets: Eli Beard led the Dragons with 21 points and 7 rebounds.
Drexel outscored Northeastern 39-22 in the first half.
Northeastern’s Mike Loughnane scored 29 points in his season finale.
The Huskies have now lost 12 consecutive games.
Drexel advances to play No. 4 seed Monmouth on Sunday.
games.context: Drexel advances to the CAA quarterfinals.

games.headline: Neely’s record-breaking 24 rebounds lead UNCG in SoCon upset
games.brief: In an absolute battle on the glass, UNC Greensboro pulled off a major upset of No. 2 seed Wofford in the SoCon quarterfinals. Justin Neely was a force of nature, breaking his own school record with 24 rebounds to go along with 22 points. Wofford had a chance to tie it at the buzzer, but a late triple missed the mark, sending the Spartans to the semifinals.
bullets: Justin Neely set a school record with 24 rebounds in the victory.
UNC Greensboro pulled off the upset as the No. 7 seed.
Wofford’s Kahmare Holmes led all scorers with 24 points.
Noah Norgaard hit five 3-pointers for the Spartans.
The Terriers missed a game-tying 3-pointer as time expired.
games.context: UNCG continues its Cinderella run as the 7-seed in the SoCon.

games.headline: Winthrop survives late Presbyterian scare to reach Big South final
games.brief: Winthrop punched their ticket to the Big South Championship, but they had to sweat until the final second to do it. After leading by eight late, the Eagles watched Presbyterian roar back to within two, only to survive a potential game-winning miss at the buzzer. Kody Clouet provided the offensive punch for Winthrop as they move one win away from the Big Dance.
bullets: Kody Clouet led the Eagles with 17 points and 4 steals.
Winthrop led 32-25 at the half.
Presbyterian’s Triston Wilson scored a game-high 26 points.
The Eagles survived a last-second shot from Presbyterian's Carl Parrish.
Winthrop will play High Point in the championship game on Sunday.
games.context: Winthrop is one win away from an automatic bid to the NCAA Tournament.

games.headline: UIC destroys Drake to reach Missouri Valley final
games.brief: UIC is headed to the Missouri Valley title game after a comprehensive blowout of Drake in St. Louis. Rashund Washington Jr. dominated the Bulldogs, scoring 23 points to lead an offense that outscored Drake by 14 in the second half. The loss is the ninth straight for a Drake squad that completely lost its identity down the stretch of the season.
bullets: Rashund Washington Jr. scored a game-high 23 points.
Mekhi Lowery recorded 12 points, 12 rebounds, and 5 assists.
Drake has lost nine consecutive games to end their season.
UIC took the lead 4 minutes into the game and never looked back.
The Flames outscored the Bulldogs 38-24 in the second half.
games.context: UIC will play for an automatic NCAA bid on Sunday.

games.headline: Georgia explodes for 60 second-half points to set program wins record
games.brief: Georgia made history in Starkville, securing the most wins in a single season in program history with a high-octane victory over Mississippi State. The Bulldogs trailed at the half but erupted for 60 points in the final 20 minutes, fueled by a relentless 17-of-29 performance from 3-point range. Not even a 42-point masterpiece from Mississippi State's Josh Hubbard could stop Georgia’s historic night.
bullets: Georgia set a program record for most regular-season wins (22).
The Bulldogs shot a blistering 17-of-29 (58.6%) from 3-point range.
Josh Hubbard scored 42 points for Mississippi State, his 7th game over 30.
Kanon Catchings led Georgia with 23 points.
Georgia is now 8-0 this season when scoring 100+ points.
games.context: A historic regular season for Georgia as they look toward a deep SEC run.

games.headline: Fulton’s buzzer-beater sends NJIT to America East semifinals
games.brief: In a heart-stopping finish in Newark, Ari Fulton played the hero for NJIT. After a gritty back-and-forth affair, Fulton drained a bucket with just one second remaining to eliminate Maine and send the Highlanders to the America East semifinals. Sebastian Robinson provided the bulk of the offense, but it was Fulton’s late-game poise that kept NJIT’s season alive.
bullets: Ari Fulton scored the game-winning bucket with 1.0 seconds left.
Sebastian Robinson led NJIT with 21 points.
Maine's Ace Flagg had 14 points and 8 rebounds in the loss.
NJIT led 32-29 at the half.
The Highlanders advance to face No. 2 seed Vermont on Tuesday.
games.context: NJIT keeps their tournament dreams alive with a clutch victory.

games.headline: Harris dominates on the glass to lead Wake Forest's comeback over Cal
games.brief: Wake Forest closed out the ACC regular season with a signature win, rallying from a double-digit deficit to top Cal. Juke Harris was the engine for the Demon Deacons, posting a massive 31-point, 15-rebound double-double. Cal jumped out to an early 13-point lead, but a 14-0 second-half run by Wake Forest flipped the momentum and secured the win for the home crowd.
bullets: Juke Harris tallied 31 points and a career-high 15 rebounds.
Wake Forest out-rebounded Cal 45-29 in the victory.
Cal’s Dai Dai Ames matched the high score with 31 points of his own.
The Demon Deacons used a 14-0 run midway through the second half to take control.
Cal led by as many as 13 points in the first half.
games.context: Wake Forest earns the No. 12 seed and faces Virginia Tech in the ACC first round.

games.headline: Pitt clinches ACC tournament spot with overtime thriller at Syracuse
games.brief: Pittsburgh secured their postseason life in the most dramatic way possible, winning a defensive overtime grind at the Carrier Dome. Nojus Indrusaitis provided the deciding basket via a goaltending call with just 4 seconds left in the extra period. The win officially punches Pitt’s ticket to the ACC Tournament, while Syracuse enters the postseason on a concerning five-game losing streak.
bullets: Nojus Indrusaitis scored the winning bucket with 4.3 seconds left in OT.
Cameron Corhen led Pitt with 21 points.
The win clinches the final spot in the ACC Tournament for Pittsburgh.
Syracuse guard Naithan George scored a season-high 26 points.
The game was tied 65-65 at the end of regulation.
games.context: Pitt clinches the 15-seed and will face Stanford in the opening round.

games.headline: Scott Drew collects 500th win as Baylor routs Utah
games.brief: Baylor coach Scott Drew joined an elite club, securing his 500th career victory as the Bears dismantled Utah. The game was a total offensive showcase for Baylor, who shot over 61% from the field and forced 16 turnovers. Freshman Tounde Yessoufou led the way with 26 points as the Bears built a 20-point halftime lead and never looked back, celebrating their coach's historic milestone in style.
bullets: Scott Drew becomes one of only 25 active D-I coaches with 500 career wins.
Baylor shot a blistering 61.5% as a team.
Tounde Yessoufou led all scorers with 26 points.
Baylor forced 16 turnovers which led to 31 points.
Utah has now lost five consecutive games.
games.context: A milestone win for Scott Drew as Baylor heads into the Big 12 Tournament.

games.headline: Thornton becomes Ohio State’s all-time leading scorer in blowout of Indiana
games.brief: Bruce Thornton etched his name into Ohio State lore, passing Dennis Hopson to become the Buckeyes’ all-time leading scorer during a dominant Senior Night win. Ohio State’s offense was nearly unstoppable, shooting 59% from the field and building a 17-point lead by halftime. Indiana had no answer for the Buckeyes' perimeter attack as Ohio State locked in a double-bye for the Big Ten Tournament.
bullets: Bruce Thornton became Ohio State’s all-time leading scorer with a 25-point night.
Ohio State shot 59% from the field and 46% from deep.
The Buckeyes led by 17 points at the half and never trailed after the early minutes.
Lamar Wilkerson led the Hoosiers with 18 points.
Ohio State clinched a double-bye for the Big Ten Tournament.
games.context: A historic night in Columbus as Ohio State cements its status as a Big Ten power.

games.headline: Tejada’s 35-point explosion carries Towson past Hampton
games.brief: Tyler Tejada put on a clinic in the CAA second round, dropping 35 points to carry Towson past a resilient Hampton squad. The Tigers took a double-digit lead into the half and leaned on Tejada's scoring whenever Hampton threatened to close the gap. The win moves the 7th-seeded Tigers into a Sunday matchup against the conference powerhouse, Charleston.
bullets: Tyler Tejada scored a game-high 35 points.
Towson led 30-17 at the half.
Hampton’s Michael Eley led his team with 23 points.
The Tigers shot a strong percentage to hold off the late Hampton surge.
Towson advances to face second-seeded Charleston.
games.context: Towson keeps its CAA tournament run alive behind a career performance from Tejada.

games.headline: Furman upsets Samford as House provides spark off the bench
games.brief: Furman pulled off one of the biggest upsets of the SoCon second round, toppling 3rd-seeded Samford behind a massive effort from reserve Tom House. The game was a shootout that saw the teams tied at the break before House’s 15 second-half points helped the Paladins pull away. Samford’s Jadin Booth put up a heroic 34 points, but it wasn't enough to stop the balanced Paladin attack.
bullets: Tom House scored 20 points off the bench for Furman.
Samford’s Jadin Booth led all scorers with 34 points.
The teams were tied 46-46 at halftime.
Furman shot a near-perfect 7-of-7 from the line late to seal it.
Alex Wilkins added 19 points for the victorious Paladins.
games.context: Furman advances to the SoCon semifinals with major upset momentum.

games.headline: Northern Iowa upsets Bradley to reach Missouri Valley final
games.brief: Northern Iowa is one win away from the NCAA Tournament after a gritty upset victory over No. 2 seed Bradley. Leon Bond III was the difference-maker, missing only one shot all night on his way to 19 points. The Panthers led by double digits at the half and maintained their composure as Bradley threw everything they had at them in the closing minutes.
bullets: Leon Bond III scored 19 points on an efficient 7-of-8 shooting.
Northern Iowa led 45-32 at the half.
The Panthers forced several late turnovers to preserve the lead.
Northern Iowa advances to face UIC in the championship game.
Bradley was held to just 21 total field goals in the loss.
games.context: Northern Iowa and UIC will face off on Sunday for an automatic NCAA berth.

games.headline: Menard’s late triple sends Marist to MAAC semifinals
games.brief: In a back-and-forth MAAC quarterfinal, Marist’s Justin Menard provided the definitive blow with a clutch 3-pointer in the final minutes. Menard’s 26 points paced a Red Fox offense that surrendered a double-digit halftime lead before digging deep late. Quinnipiac got a massive 28-point, 15-rebound game from Grant Randall, but his late efforts couldn't prevent the Bobcats' early exit.
bullets: Justin Menard led Marist with 26 points and 6 assists.
The Red Foxes used a late 3-pointer with 2:07 left to take the lead for good.
Quinnipiac’s Grant Randall had a dominant 28-point, 15-rebound double-double.
Marist led 42-32 at the half.
The Red Foxes advance to the MAAC semifinals in Atlantic City.
games.context: Marist continues its push for a MAAC title and NCAA berth.

games.headline: Iwuchukwu’s double-double helps Georgetown snap seven-game skid
games.brief: Georgetown finally found the win column on the final day of the Big East regular season, edging Providence in a one-point thriller. Vincent Iwuchukwu was dominant in the paint, posting 25 points and 10 rebounds while providing the go-ahead dunk late in the second half. The Hoyas' defense held firm in the final minute to end a frustrating seven-game losing streak.
bullets: Vincent Iwuchukwu tallied 25 points and 10 rebounds.
Georgetown snapped a season-worst seven-game losing streak.
Providence’s Jaylin Sellers led the Friars with 21 points.
The Hoyas outscored the Friars by 9 points in the first half.
The win gives Georgetown much-needed momentum for the Big East Tournament.
games.context: Georgetown picks up their 14th win of the year to end the regular season.

games.headline: Western Carolina wins seventh straight to reach SoCon semifinals
games.brief: Western Carolina is the hottest team in the Southern Conference, extending their winning streak to seven games by fending off Mercer. Tidjiane Dioumassi paced the Catamounts with 21 points, while Marcus Kell provided the steady hand at the free-throw line to seal it in the final 30 seconds. The win sets up a heavyweight semifinal matchup against top-seeded East Tennessee State.
bullets: Tidjiane Dioumassi led WCU with 21 points and 4 triples.
The Catamounts have won seven consecutive games.
Mercer’s Baraka Okojie led all scorers with 26 points.
Marcus Kell scored 13 of his 15 points in the second half.
WCU advances to play No. 1 seed ETSU on Sunday.
games.context: WCU looks to continue their heater against the SoCon's top seed.

games.headline: Oklahoma outlasts Texas in overtime shootout in Austin
games.brief: The final installment of the Red River Rivalry for the regular season required extra time, with Oklahoma prevailing in a high-octane overtime battle. Nijel Pack and Xzayvier Brown combined for 44 points to lead the Sooners, who fended off a late Texas rally that saw the Longhorns tie the game with three late free throws. Derrion Reid’s late 3-pointer in overtime finally put the game out of reach for Texas.
bullets: Nijel Pack led the Sooners with 23 points.
Texas’ Jordan Pope scored 30 points on 8-of-25 shooting.
The game went to overtime after Pope hit three free throws to tie it in regulation.
Derrion Reid hit the game-deciding 3-pointer with 11 seconds left in OT.
Texas has lost four of their final five games heading into the postseason.
games.context: The Sooners earn the 11-seed while Texas settles for the 10-seed in the SEC.

games.headline: Siena coasts to MAAC semifinal berth behind Coyle’s double-double
games.brief: Siena controlled their MAAC quarterfinal from the opening tip, building a 14-point halftime lead and never allowing Mount St. Mary's to seriously threaten. Brendan Coyle was the standout for the Saints, recording 17 points and 9 rebounds to anchor the victory. The Saints' defense held firm late as they advance to face Fairfield in the conference semifinals.
bullets: Brendan Coyle led Siena with 17 points and 9 rebounds.
Siena led 35-21 at the half.
Gavin Doty added 17 points, including a perfect 10-of-10 from the line.
Mount St. Mary's Luke McEldon scored 18 points in the loss.
The Saints advance to a Sunday semifinal against Fairfield.
games.context: Siena continues their push for a MAAC title in Atlantic City.

games.headline: Fuchs’ monster double-double powers San Francisco past Portland
games.brief: San Francisco used a dominant second-half performance to roll past Portland and advance in the WCC Tournament. David Fuchs was a force on both ends, tallying 23 points and a massive 16 rebounds to lead the Dons. After a tied first half, USF’s defense clamped down and Tyrone Riley IV took over the scoring duties to secure a 17-point margin of victory.
bullets: David Fuchs recorded 23 points and 16 rebounds.
San Francisco outscored Portland by 17 points in the second half.
The game was tied 29-29 at the half.
Tyrone Riley IV scored all 18 of his points in the second half.
The Dons advance to play No. 4 seed Oregon State on Sunday.
games.context: San Francisco advances to the WCC quarterfinals in Paradise.

games.headline: UCLA sets Galen Center scoring record in blowout of rival Trojans
games.brief: UCLA continued its dominance of the crosstown rivalry, routing USC for the second time in 11 days. Donovan Dent was the star once again, following up a 30-point performance last week with 25 points and 7 assists to lead the Bruins to their highest-ever point total at Galen Center. For the Trojans, the loss is their seventh in a row to end a disappointing regular season characterized by late-season collapses and roster departures.
bullets: UCLA set a school record for points scored at the Galen Center (89).
Donovan Dent led the Bruins with 25 points on 11-of-15 shooting.
USC ended the regular season with seven consecutive losses.
The Bruins outscored the Trojans by 15 points in the second half.
This was UCLA's largest road win in the rivalry since 1974.
games.context: UCLA enters the Big Ten Tournament with momentum, while USC's season spirals.

games.headline: Reynolds’ late-game heroics lead Minnesota past Wildcats
games.brief: Langston Reynolds played the hero for the Golden Gophers, scoring the final four points in the closing seconds to snatch a win from Northwestern. After trailing by three late, Reynolds converted a clutch layup with 11 seconds left to seal the comeback. The Wildcats were led by Nick Martinelli’s 23 points, but they couldn't overcome Minnesota’s balanced attack and Reynolds’ 17-point second half.
bullets: Langston Reynolds led the Gophers with 21 points, including 17 in the second half.
Minnesota opened the game on a dominant 18-2 run.
Northwestern’s Nick Martinelli scored a game-high 23 points.
Reynolds scored the final four points of the game in the last 26 seconds.
Minnesota secured the No. 11 seed in the Big Ten Tournament.
games.context: Minnesota builds momentum for a Wednesday conference tournament start.

games.headline: Idaho cruises past Sacramento State to reach Big Sky quarterfinals
games.brief: Idaho left no room for doubt in their Big Sky Tournament opener, taking a lead early and never looking back. Jackson Rasmussen led the Vandals with 19 points as they dismantled a Sacramento State offense that struggled to find any rhythm. The Hornets' season ends on a difficult eight-game losing streak, while the Vandals advance to face No. 2 seed Montana State.
bullets: Jackson Rasmussen led Idaho with 19 points.
Sacramento State was held to just 13 points in the first half.
Idaho used a dominant 11-0 run early in the second half to seal it.
The Vandals out-rebounded the Hornets 35-24.
Sacramento State finishes the season with eight consecutive losses.
games.context: Idaho advances to a Sunday quarterfinal matchup.

games.headline: Nevada hands Air Force its 24th consecutive loss in wire-to-wire rout
games.brief: Nevada dominated from the opening tip, never trailing in a comfortable win over Air Force. Corey Camper Jr. led the Wolf Pack with 18 points as they pulled away with a decisive 12-2 second-half run. For the Falcons, the loss extends the nation's longest-active losing streak to a staggering 24 games, ending a winless Mountain West campaign on a low note.
bullets: Air Force has now lost 24 consecutive games.
Corey Camper Jr. scored a team-high 18 points.
Nevada took the lead 23 seconds into the game and never surrendered it.
The Wolf Pack finished the Mountain West regular season at 12-8.
Air Force was held to just 25 points in the first half.
games.context: Nevada finishes with a 20-win season while Air Force's record slide continues.

games.headline: Evans’ late-game heroics lead Oregon to miraculous comeback over Huskies
games.brief: Oregon looked dead in the water after blowing a 21-point lead, but Kwame Evans Jr. had other plans. Evans scored the game’s final nine points, including a clutch and-1 to take the lead with 11 seconds left. The finish was marred by chaos as Washington coach Danny Sprinkle was ejected following a controversial non-call, allowing Evans to seal the victory at the free-throw line.
bullets: Kwame Evans Jr. scored 20 points, including the game's final nine points.
Washington coach Danny Sprinkle was ejected with five seconds remaining.
Oregon blew a 21-point first-half lead before rallying late.
Hannes Steinbach led the Huskies with 26 points and 13 rebounds.
The Ducks shot 7-of-7 from 3-point range to start the game.
games.context: Oregon survives a scare to end the regular season with a win.
